hero.title: Eagle Day
hero.reason: In honor of World Bicycle Day, Eagle Day is a film that showcases the beauty of cycling and the freedom it brings, making it a great fit for this day.
hero.blurb: Eagle Day is a visually stunning film that will take you on a journey through breathtaking landscapes, with a strong narrative that explores the human connection with nature. The cinematography is impressive, and the storyline is both inspiring and emotional.

released.title: Knocked Up
released.reason: Released today in 2007, Knocked Up is a hilarious and heartwarming film that still holds up today, thanks to its relatable characters and witty dialogue.
released.blurb: Knocked Up is a classic romantic comedy that explores the unexpected consequences of a one-night stand, featuring standout performances from Seth Rogen and Katherine Heigl. The film's blend of humor and sentimentality makes it a must-watch.

birthday.title: Jefferson in Paris
birthday.reason: Happy birthday to James Earl Jones, Jefferson in Paris is a historical drama film that showcases his exceptional acting skills, bringing to life the complex character of Madison Hemings.
birthday.blurb: Jefferson in Paris is a thought-provoking film that explores the life of Thomas Jefferson, with James Earl Jones delivering a powerful performance. The film's historical context and stunning cinematography make it a compelling watch.

awful.title: Ballistic: Ecks vs. Sever
awful.reason: This notoriously bad action film has gained a cult following for its over-the-top antics and cringe-worthy dialogue, making it a hilarious guilty pleasure.
awful.blurb: Ballistic: Ecks vs. Sever is a mind-bogglingly bad film that will leave you questioning the life choices of its cast and crew. With its nonsensical plot and atrocious acting, it's a mesmerizing trainwreck that's impossible to look away from.
